headline: SLH-DSA object identifiers corrected and completed to all twelve FIPS 205 parameter sets.
summary: The SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) signature-algorithm OIDs under the NIST sigAlgs arc (2.16.840.1.101.3.4.3) were incomplete and two were mislabelled: id-slh-dsa-shake-128s and id-slh-dsa-shake-256s were registered at arcs .24 and .27, which are actually id-slh-dsa-sha2-256s and id-slh-dsa-shake-128f. As a result pki.oid.name resolved those two arcs to the wrong parameter-set name, pki.oid.byName returned the wrong OID for those names, and the other nine parameter sets were unregistered. All twelve Pure SLH-DSA parameter sets are now registered at their correct sequential arcs per RFC 9909 §3 (the SHA-2 sets at .20-.25 and the SHAKE sets at .26-.31), so every id-slh-dsa-* name round-trips to its exact OID. This is a naming-resolution fix in the OID registry; the WebCrypto signing and verification paths select SLH-DSA by algorithm name and were unaffected.

Fixed: SLH-DSA OID resolution — id-slh-dsa-shake-128s and id-slh-dsa-shake-256s were mapped to the arcs of id-slh-dsa-sha2-256s (.24) and id-slh-dsa-shake-128f (.27), so pki.oid.name / pki.oid.byName resolved them incorrectly. All twelve Pure SLH-DSA parameter sets (sha2-128s/128f/192s/192f/256s/256f and shake-128s/128f/192s/192f/256s/256f) are now registered at their correct arcs .20 through .31 per RFC 9909 §3; the previously-absent nine sets now resolve as well. WebCrypto SLH-DSA sign/verify was unaffected — it selects by algorithm name, not through the OID registry.
